description_long: This object is a realistic steak with an irregular, roughly triangular shape and a rugged, uneven surface. The exterior is predominantly a deep reddish-brown with mottled patches of lighter pink, yellow, and some darker brown, simulating the appearance of cooked meat. There is a small, white label with the text 'TC 26' affixed to one side, suggesting it may be cataloged or part of a collection. The surface texture appears fibrous and slightly glossy in some areas, with no visible grill marks or charring. The steak does not display any visible bone or garnish, and the marbling of fat is light and sporadically distributed. Its size and form suggest it could be found as discarded food or a prop in an urban environment.
description: A rugged, reddish-brown steak with a fibrous, uneven surface and a small white label, showing light fat marbling and no visible bone or grill marks.
description_view_0: This view highlights the steak's elongated, pointed end and the deep reddish-brown coloration with subtle marbling.
description_view_1: This angle shows the broadest side of the steak, featuring the white 'TC 26' label and a more pronounced mix of red, pink, and yellow hues.
description_view_2: The label 'TC 26' is clearly visible here, with the surface showing more texture and a slightly more compact, blocky appearance.
description_view_3: This side displays the steak's narrow, tapered edge and a smoother, less detailed surface with darker coloration.
attribute_grill_marks: No grill marks are visible on the surface of the steak.
attribute_visible_fat_marbling: Light fat marbling is present, distributed sporadically across the steak.
